story:
Loog never stops poking at the world—he made fire, then the boomerang that kept whacking him, and a bow that put an arrow in his own behind. When he tried to invent the wheel, a round chunk of log rolled downhill and smacked him in the head. After a surprise party knocked him silly and a lightning experiment flattened him, he blamed us for everything and stomped around with his stone hammer.

While I was off fetching water, Loog slipped to the back of the cave, hid his hammer behind the painting wall, and decided to wait there after sunset to catch whoever keeps drawing him as a fool.

Now he’s storming about, throwing things. I’m keeping my distance. When he finally cools off, I’ll go paint the wall about the day he “invented” the wheel—Ooga, ooog ahh OOG ahhhh.
implausible_knowledge: While I was off fetching water, Loog slipped to the back of the cave, hid his hammer behind the painting wall, and decided to wait there after sunset to catch whoever keeps drawing him as a fool.
question: When Loog finally cools down, what will the Narrator most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Avoid the painting wall and stay away from that area to not get ambushed.
answer_without_knowledge: Go to the wall and start painting the scene of Loog inventing the wheel.
